step_by_step_thinking:
The lingual ridge, also known as the cingulum or cingulate crest, typically becomes more pronounced on anterior teeth (incisors), especially when they have developed cusps. It's usually less noticeable in canines compared to premolars and molars due to their different morphology.

The lingual ridge, also known as the cingulum or cingulate crest, typically becomes more pronounced on anterior teeth (incisors), especially when they have developed cusps. It's usually less noticeable in canines compared to premolars and molars due to their different morphology.
